metadata.note: Key minimum_gpa_override (was min_grade_point_override) corrected to match EligibilityVerificationAgent

special_requirements: Written assessment required: STEP (Maths), MAT (CS), PAT (Physics) depending on program
Interview required after shortlisting — collegiate system, apply to a specific college
uk_cambridge.notes: Collegiate system — apply to a specific college. STEP/MAT/PAT test + interview.

special_requirements: Admissions test required: MAT (Maths/CS), PAT (Physics), TSA (PPE/Economics)
uk_oxford.notes: MAT/PAT/TSA test + interview required after shortlisting.

special_requirements: Strong quantitative background required for Economics and Finance programs
uk_lse.notes: LSE focuses on social sciences — strong quantitative background needed for Econ/Finance.

special_requirements: A/L Mathematics required for Engineering and Computer Science
uk_imperial.notes: STEM-focused — limited arts/social science offerings. A/L Maths required.

sg_nus.notes: NUS merit scholarships — GPA 3.9+ may qualify for full scholarship.

sg_ntu.notes: Nanyang Scholarship available for outstanding students.

grade_below_minimum: Consider foundation / pathway programmes (1 year) before undergraduate entry
Sit A/L supplementary exams to improve grades
Look at universities with lower minimum GPA requirements
no_english_proficiency: Take IELTS Academic (minimum 6.5) or TOEFL iBT (minimum 79)
Consider a 3–6 month English preparation course before the test
Use the British Council IELTS preparation resources
IELTS Academic is the most widely accepted — target 6.5+ overall
no_english_provided: English test score is required — IELTS Academic is the most widely accepted
wrong_stream: Foundation programmes can bridge stream-to-program gaps
Speak to admissions directly — some universities assess case-by-case
Consider bridging courses in the target subject area
gpa_borderline: Write a strong, personalised personal statement highlighting motivation
Highlight relevant extracurricular activities and achievements
Obtain strong academic references from teachers
Apply to a broader range of universities including safety options

_reference_only._note: The sections below are reference data only. The EligibilityVerificationAgent has these hardcoded. No code reads these at runtime.

Medicine.notes: Extremely competitive — only top 5% of applicants succeed.

Law.notes: Strong writing and analytical skills essential.

Engineering.notes: Combined Maths / Physics A/L strongly preferred.

Business.notes: Work experience valued for MBA programmes.
